text: In the 1950s, sleep researcher Nathaniel Kleitman — who had earlier discovered REM sleep — noticed that the 90-minute cycles marking deep and light sleep did not vanish when people woke up. They continued throughout the day as what he called the Basic Rest-Activity Cycle, or BRAC. Every 90 minutes or so, the brain completes a full oscillation between focused alertness and a quieter, more diffuse state. Most people override this signal with caffeine or willpower, but the biology persists regardless. The drowsiness, the wandering attention, the urge to stare out a window — these are not failures of discipline. They are the nervous system requesting its scheduled maintenance.
question: According to the passage, what did Kleitman call the recurring 90-minute cycles he observed during waking hours?
